text.title: rust-error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-error-handling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: fn process() -> Result<(), String> returning Err("something went wrong".to_string()).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Caller cannot distinguish error kinds. Forces string matching which is fragile and slow.

failure-mode.description: unwrap() or expect() in production async fn "to keep it short".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any None or Err panics the entire Tokio task, crashing all concurrent requests sharing that thread. Use ? and propagate instead.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The save fails invisibly. At minimum, log the error; for data integrity paths, propagate with ?.

failure-mode.description: Adding #[from] anyhow::Error to a thiserror enum variant in a library.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Collapses all downstream errors into one opaque variant. Callers lose the ability to match on specific failure types.

failure-mode.description: From<&str> or From<String> impls on error enums.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These silently swallow context. Use #[from] on real error types only, not on string messages.

failure-mode.description: IntoResponse returning 500 for every AppError variant.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hides NotFound (should be 404), Unauthorized (401), etc. Clients cannot act on the response. Require per-variant status mapping.
